raw_consumer_statement:
On XX/XX/XXXX, I noticed an unauthorized charge of {$45.00} on my American Express card, listed as XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX, XXXX - XX/XX/year>. This charge is incorrect, as I am currently not in XXXX and did not rent a vehicle in late XXXX. I immediately contacted XXXX, but I was unable to obtain any information from their overseas representatives about this transaction. 

In response, I initiated a dispute with American Express ( Dispute ID : XXXX ), expecting their support in resolving what is clearly an unauthorized charge. American Express informed me that they sent an email on XX/XX/XXXX regarding the outcome of their investigation ; however, I never received this email and, to date, have not been provided with any explanation or documentation justifying the charge. 

In addition, American Express suggested that I follow up on my own, which is not feasible given that I am not in XXXX and have not rented a vehicle. This response is unsatisfactory and fails to address my need for a resolution. The charge is both unauthorized and incorrect, and I have taken all reasonable steps to notify American Express of this issue. 

I am seeking the CFPBs assistance to ensure that American Express provides a full refund of the {$45.00} charge, along with a complete and transparent explanation of how this charge was allowed to occur despite my lack of involvement and absence from XXXX.
